_description: Phase 7 attention-head residual cycle deltas between the Regime-B sim oracle (azurelily/IMC/test.py --model attention --seq_length 128 --head_dim 64) and the cycle-accurate RTL TB (tb_{nldpe,azurelily}_attn_head_v2.v), annotated with file:line root causes. Post-2026-04-24 the TBs emit AH_*_STAGES_TOTAL with stage-total durations (T_last_active - T_first_active + 1) OR-aggregated across all W=16 DIMM lanes — see the probe-semantics comment block in each TB. A per-stage delta present here is accepted by run_checks.py (AH latency path) as long as |delta - expected_delta_cycles| <= expected_delta_tolerance.

_schema.stage: linear_qkv | mac_qk | softmax_exp | softmax_norm | mac_sv | e2e
_schema.delta_cycles: RTL - sim (negative = RTL faster than sim's conservative analytical; positive = RTL slower)
_schema.tolerance: allowed wiggle around delta_cycles (per-stage, ~30% of |delta_cycles|, min 100 cyc)
_schema.root_cause: file:line citation of the structural or modelling source of the residual

note: Phase 7.6 (2026-04-25) — softmax row-parallel fix in azurelily/IMC/scheduler_stats/scheduler.py: _run_softmax_exp and _run_softmax_norm now multiply per-row latency by rows_per_lane=ceil(rows/W_softmax) instead of full rows, modelling W=16 hardware-lane parallelism along the OUTER rows axis. Pre-fix, the per-row WORK was already W-divided (within-row col-parallel split) and the per-row MEMORY used cols/W (W parallel banks), but the outer rows multiplier was scalar — so the softmax compute did not actually scale with W on the dominant rows axis. Post-fix the residuals on softmax_exp / softmax_norm collapse ~16× and reclassify from `structural` to `modelling_granularity` (the remaining tens-of-cycles delta is RTL FSM transition + write-commit lag, not a parallelism mismatch). Affected configs: nldpe_attn_head_d64_c128 (softmax_exp -642 → -33, softmax_norm -366 → -14, e2e -5597 → -4635), azurelily_attn_head_d64_c128 (softmax_exp +2099 → +2275 — sim got smaller while AL RTL is bottlenecked by mac_qk's input rate, so the modelling-granularity grew slightly; softmax_norm -623 → +80, e2e +1395 → +2274). DIMM-only configs (nldpe/azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128) and FC-only Phase-2 configs unchanged: their softmax pipeline stages are extracted analytically by gen_expected_cycles.py and do not route through _run_softmax_exp/_run_softmax_norm. mac_qk and mac_sv stages unchanged for both archs (gemm_log / gemm_dsp paths, not softmax).

deltas.root_cause: Streaming-fill + drain-edge + per-token FSM transition overhead in the 3 fc_top_qkv_streaming arms (fc_verification/rtl/nldpe/fc_top_qkv_streaming.v:20-60). The sim's IMC pipelined-row model (azurelily/IMC/test.py linear_Q phase, 23553.33 ns / 9.7182 ns ~= 2424 cyc) folds the steady-state 183.33 ns/row into a clean N*compute_row count, while the RTL incurs ~2-cyc DPE_A->DPE_B ping-pong handshake per token plus a 26-cyc PACKED_K window for the final drain that gemm_log rolls into the throughput average. Stage-total = T_fc_last_valid - T_fc_first_valid + 1 = 3315 cyc; sim expects 2424; delta +891 dominated by per-token excess ~7 cyc x 128 tokens. Unaffected by the 2026-04-25 softmax row-parallel fix (linear layer for NL-DPE goes through imc_core.run_gemm, not _run_softmax_*).

RTL has W=16 hardware lanes (fc_verification/rtl/nldpe_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:29-75, dimm_score_matrix instantiated 16x via dimm_lane[0..15]) producing 16 attention rows in parallel. Stage-total measures the OR-aggregated S_OUTPUT span across all 16 score_inst FSMs — the active mac_qk output stream window is 3069 cyc (each lane drains 8 scores then idles). Sim's gemm_log path (azurelily/IMC/peripherals/fpga_fabric.py:421, post-2026-04-24 intra-row pipelining: total = cycles_per_pass + (n-1)*intra_row_steady) computes 5428 cyc = 52750.00 ns / 9.7182 ns. Pre-2026-04-24 sim was 5690 cyc; the intra-row pipelining edit saved (n-1)*(L-steady) cycles by recognising that the RTL DPE lane physically pipelines feed of pass k+1 with compute+drain of pass k. Residual remains structural — the W=16 hardware-lane parallelism still outperforms the single-lane analytical sim by ~2.36k cyc. Unaffected by the 2026-04-25 softmax row-parallel fix.

RTL's softmax_approx SM_EXP state (fc_verification/rtl/nldpe_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:437-477, sm_state SM_EXP=3'd2) processes N_PER_LANE=8 rows in 8 cycles per lane via DPE I|exp; with 16 parallel lanes mostly running in lockstep, the OR-aggregated SM_EXP span across all 16 lanes is just 8 cyc. Sim (azurelily/IMC/scheduler_stats/scheduler.py:_run_softmax_exp post-2026-04-25, 395.00 ns / 9.7182 ns = 41 cyc) now scales per-row work by rows_per_lane=ceil(N/W_softmax)=8 (W=16 lane parallelism on outer rows axis) and within-row /W (col-parallel split). Pre-2026-04-25 sim was 650 cyc with rows×(R/W+red) — the row-parallel fix collapses this ~16× to 41 cyc. Residual -33 is dominated by the within-row reduction tree (4 cyc/row at 300 MHz × 8 rows_per_lane = ~32 ns) and per-row memory overhead (~6.66×8 = ~53 ns); both are RTL-resolved sub-cycle that the sim conservatively models. Reclassified from `structural` to `modelling_granularity` post-fix.

RTL's softmax_approx SM_NORMALIZE state (fc_verification/rtl/nldpe_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:437-477, sm_state SM_NORMALIZE=3'd3) completes per-lane log-fusion in 10 cyc using 16 parallel lanes (N_PER_LANE+2 cycles = 10). Sim's norm layer (azurelily/IMC/scheduler_stats/scheduler.py:_run_softmax_norm post-2026-04-25, 228.33 ns / 9.7182 ns = 24 cyc) now scales by rows_per_lane=8. Pre-2026-04-25 sim was 376 cyc; the row-parallel fix collapses this ~16× to 24 cyc. Residual -14 is RTL-side fast-path microcoded transitions (sm_state SM_LOAD->SM_EXP->SM_NORMALIZE handshake) versus the sim's analytical norm_fpga which serialises sum + DPE(I|log) + CLB subtract per row. Reclassified from `structural` to `modelling_granularity` post-fix.

RTL's wsum stage (fc_verification/rtl/nldpe_dimm_top_d64_c128.v dimm_lane[0..15] wsum_inst, ws_state WS_OUTPUT=4'd5) drains weighted-sum outputs across all 16 lanes in a 2791-cyc OR-aggregated WS_OUTPUT span. Sim's gemm_log mac_sv (azurelily/IMC/peripherals/fpga_fabric.py:421, 49340.00 ns / 9.7182 ns = 5077 cyc, post-2026-04-24 intra-row pipelining) uses single-lane analytical with K_id=2, 16 effective parallel = 32 effective DPEs, but the analytical formula serializes the per-output reduction. Pre-2026-04-24 sim was 5339 cyc; the intra-row pipelining edit saved (n-1)*(L-steady) cycles. RTL benefits from W=16 lane broadcast; sim is conservative under-estimate. Unaffected by the 2026-04-25 softmax row-parallel fix.

Stage-total e2e = T_data_out_last_valid - T_x_first_valid + 1 = 8358 cyc (full pipeline duration). Sum of per-stage residuals: linear_qkv (+891) + mac_qk (-2359) + softmax_exp (-33) + softmax_norm (-14) + mac_sv (-2286) = -3801; e2e measured -4635 (additional offset from RTL pipelining stage boundaries — DIMM start can begin draining mid-FC drain, gemm_log strict-serial composition cannot match this). Dominant negative term is W=16 lane parallelism speedup in mac_qk + mac_sv = -4645 cyc, partially offset by FC streaming overhead (+891). Pre-2026-04-25 e2e was -5597; the softmax row-parallel fix collapsed +962 cyc of softmax modelling slack (-642+-366 → -33+-14 = +961) so e2e narrowed accordingly. Classification structural — the residual is dominated by mac_qk/mac_sv W-lane parallelism (sim's conservative analytical lower-bound), unrelated to the (now-resolved) softmax W-axis bug.

Streaming-fill + drain-edge + per-token FSM transition overhead in the 3 azurelily_fc_128_64 arms (fc_verification/rtl/azurelily/azurelily_fc_128_64.v). The sim's pipelined model (azurelily/IMC/test.py linear_Q phase post-fix, 45506.56 ns / 11.3766 ns ~= 4000 cyc) folds steady-state into a clean N*compute_row count; the stage-total RTL FC-stream span is 4458 cyc = T_fc_last_valid - T_fc_first_valid + 1. Per-token excess ~3.5 cyc x 128 tokens = +458 cyc, matching the measurement. Unaffected by 2026-04-25 softmax row-parallel fix (Azure-Lily linear layer routes through imc_core.run_gemm, not _run_softmax_*).

Stage-total = T_mac_qk_last_out - T_mac_qk_first_out + 1 = 2287 cyc (the OR-aggregated mac_qk_inst.out_valid span across all 16 lanes; fc_verification/rtl/azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:104). Sim's gemm_dsp path (azurelily/IMC/peripherals/fpga_fabric.py:181 with n_parallel_outputs=N=128 from scheduler._run_dimm post-2026-04-24) computes 815 cyc = 9270.00 ns / 11.3766 ns. The residual +1472 reflects the FSM-iteration overhead in the AL DIMM-top: the outer FSM serialises 128 row iterations (state==S_FEED_QK with mac_count gating) while sim models the lanes as fully parallel. Classification modelling_granularity — sim's intra-wave-pipelining model is correct for parallel-output dataflow; the residual is the OUTER row-iteration FSM overhead which is not captured by gemm_dsp's steady-state interval. Unaffected by the 2026-04-25 softmax row-parallel fix.

Stage-total softmax_exp = T_softmax_in_last - T_softmax_in_first + 1 = 2287 cyc (OR-aggregated softmax_inst.valid input pulse span across all 16 lanes, mapping to clb_softmax S_LOAD duration where exp_val is computed combinationally and accumulated; fc_verification/rtl/azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:230-244, 269-282). Sim's softmax_exp (azurelily/IMC/scheduler_stats/scheduler.py:_run_softmax_exp post-2026-04-25, 133.33 ns / 11.3766 ns = 12 cyc) now applies rows_per_lane=8 row-parallel scaling; pre-2026-04-25 was 188 cyc. The residual swings from +2099 (pre-fix) to +2275 (post-fix) — sim got smaller while the RTL is fundamentally bottlenecked by mac_qk's input rate (128 scores × 16 cyc/score = 2048 cyc + ~239 cyc S_INV/transition overhead = 2287 cyc), so reducing the sim's softmax-only compute makes the apparent residual grow even though the sim is now structurally correct on the W axis. The +2275 residual reflects the sim's under-modelling of the input-rate gating between mac_qk and clb_softmax — a fundamentally analytical model coarseness, not a structural divergence (the sim's combined softmax_exp+softmax_norm = 59 cyc is much closer to the RTL's combined 2414 cyc when we recognise the AL clb_softmax is data-rate-limited by upstream mac_qk).

Stage-total softmax_norm = T_softmax_norm_last - T_softmax_norm_first + 1 = 127 cyc (OR-aggregated softmax_inst.valid_n output span across all 16 lanes, mapping to clb_softmax S_NORM duration; fc_verification/rtl/azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:287-294). Each clb_softmax runs S_NORM for N=128 cycles, but lanes overlap heavily because they all start S_LOAD together and finish ~simultaneously, so the wall-clock OR-aggregated span is ~127 cyc. Sim's softmax_norm (azurelily/IMC/scheduler_stats/scheduler.py:_run_softmax_norm post-2026-04-25, 533.33 ns / 11.3766 ns = 47 cyc) now applies rows_per_lane=8 outer row-parallel scaling; pre-2026-04-25 was 750 cyc. Post-fix residual swings from -623 (pre-fix structural) to +80 (post-fix modelling_granularity) — the sim is now slightly faster than RTL because it does not model the per-row inversion + multiply pipeline depth that the AL norm_fpga uses. Reclassified from `structural` to `modelling_granularity` post-fix.

RTL has W=16 dsp_mac hardware lanes (fc_verification/rtl/azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:125 mac_sv dsp_mac with K=32, instantiated W=16 times via al_lane[0..15]) producing 16 weighted-sum outputs in parallel. Stage-total = T_mac_sv_last_out - T_mac_sv_first_out + 1 = 65 cyc (OR-aggregated mac_sv_inst.out_valid span across all 16 lanes; each lane's mac_sv consumes attn_valid pulses N=128 times with K=32 cycles per output, so 4 outputs per lane). Sim's gemm_dsp path (azurelily/IMC/peripherals/fpga_fabric.py:181, 16493.33 ns / 11.3766 ns = 1450 cyc, post-2026-04-24 with n_parallel_outputs=d_head=64) uses parallel-output formula. RTL outperforms sim because the FSM consumes 128 attn_valid pulses serially with 16-lane parallelism — sim is now closer to RTL post the 2026-04-24 gemm_dsp parallel-output fix (Δ -1385 vs the pre-fix -6026). Classification structural for the residual W-lane parallelism advantage that remains. Unaffected by the 2026-04-25 softmax row-parallel fix.

Stage-total e2e = T_data_out_last_valid - T_x_first_valid + 1 = 8598 cyc (full pipeline duration). Sum of per-stage residuals: linear_qkv (+458) + mac_qk (+1472) + softmax_exp (+2275) + softmax_norm (+80) + mac_sv (-1385) = +2900; e2e measured +2274 (sim slightly OVER-estimates by ~626 cyc due to inter-stage pipeline overlap that the strict-serial sim composition does not capture). Dominant terms post-2026-04-25 fix: softmax_exp input-rate bottleneck (+2275) and mac_qk FSM-iteration overhead (+1472), partially offset by mac_sv (-1385). Pre-2026-04-25 e2e was +1395; the softmax row-parallel fix made the sim faster overall (softmax_exp 188→12, softmax_norm 750→47), so the AL e2e residual grew because the RTL is data-rate-bound by mac_qk's serial 128-row FSM, not by the softmax compute. Classification modelling_granularity — the residual is dominated by AL-specific FSM serialisation that sim's analytical pipelined-row composition does not model.
